creator: grahamperrin@freebsd.org
text:
Bug 255318 is /doc/ -specific, for the FreeBSD Handbook. 

This bug 253364 is broader, some overlap with /src/


/usr/src/UPDATING
=================

From <https://github.com/freebsd/freebsd-src/commit/8fc257994d0ce2396196d7a06d50d20c8015f4b7#diff-7a99811ebf6b6268a2581fcf050df0a2c325fdbf1c02481dad16afd38f4bd6a1R976-R982> (2010-07-13): 

> … always … 
> 
> …
> 
> 2.) update the ZFS boot block on your boot drive …

<https://github.com/freebsd/freebsd-src/commit/d8e59012e29dee301a7af5c2a70749637319fafc> (2020-10-31) improved the example for updating the boot block. Notably: 

* there's use of gpart(8). 

<https://github.com/freebsd/freebsd-doc/commit/f8c8bd998a6ba110e77c0702ee12e775939d66a0#diff-0df1a5ca372061e7a0c4a25a4be96491af975c5dd19bd1b50eed5ca7d15d1997R214> (2021-03-15, for D29278 by jhb@): 

> To update old ESP partitions, users should stop using the 
> man:gpart[8] utility.

– if I understand correctly, it's no longer appropriate to _always_ use gpart to update the boot block.
